name: The Hermitage of Santa Barbara — Circular via the Via Augusta
name_original: La Ermita de Santa Barbara — Circular por la Vía Augusta
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tírig offers routes through the distinctive landscape of L'Alt Maestrat in Castellón. The region features impressive cliffs, extensive almond and olive groves, and varied topography. Notable natural features include the Barranc de la Valltorta, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and surrounding hills like Sierra de Valdancha. This diverse terrain provides engaging backdrops for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Tírig The most popular touring cycling route is The Hermitage of Santa Barbara — Circular via the Via Augusta, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views towards the Hermitage of Santa Barbara. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Mateu – View of Cervera del Maestre loop from Sant Mateu, a difficult 55.9 miles (90.0 km) path. This route provides extensive views, including towards Cervera del Maestre. Local touring cyclists also love the Tiriga Motorhome Parking Area – San Mateu loop from Sant Mateu, a 41.9 miles (67.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Tírig is defined by impressive cliffs, extensive almond and olive groves, and the dramatic Barranc de la Valltorta. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Tírig are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 218 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tírig's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tírig?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available around Tírig, catering to various skill levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 25 moderate routes, and 25 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tírig?
A: Tírig benefits from an ideal climate, with winters that are not too cold and summers that are not excessively hot. This makes it conducive for outdoor pursuits, including touring cycling, throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for cycling.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners in Tírig?
A: Yes, Tírig offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 5 easy routes available. One notable option is a circular route originating from Tírig, described as being 'without too many difficulties' and ideal for those new to road cycling. An example of a moderate route that could be suitable for those looking to build experience is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17388515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Mateu – Ermita de Santa Bárbara loop from Cova dels Cavalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tírig?
A: The region boasts a distinctive and diverse landscape characterized by impressive cliffs, extensive almond and olive groves, and varied topography. You'll encounter hills and mountains, providing engaging backdrops and varied challenges for cyclists. The Barranc de la Valltorta, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, also contributes to the dramatic scenery.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Tírig?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Tírig are circular. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938355723/the-hermitage-of-santa-barbara-circular-via-the-via-augusta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Hermitage of Santa Barbara — Circular via the Via Augusta&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route offering views towards the Hermitage of Santa Barbara. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31932609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Mateu – View of Cervera del Maestre loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, a more challenging circular path.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Tírig?
A: While cycling in Tírig, you can explore several significant landmarks. The Barranc de la Valltorta is renowned for its prehistoric cave paintings, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4811270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt;, which offers splendid views from its hilltop position. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5984243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morería cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning vistas. The ride towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4811270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt; provides splendid views. Cyclists can also enjoy views of the Valltorta gorge, as well as extensive olive and almond groves, providing a scenic and engaging ride throughout the region. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31932609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Mateu – View of Cervera del Maestre loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt; are specifically noted for providing extensive views.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Tírig offers 25 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and longer distances. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38219001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Mateu – Mola Redona loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Is parking available for touring cyclists in Tírig?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Tírig is a small town with a low population density, suggesting that parking for vehicles is generally accessible within or near the village. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23753726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tiriga Motorhome Parking Area – San Mateu loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, even indicate starting points from parking facilities.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about the routes in Tírig?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tírig are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 11 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the impressive cliffs, and the extensive almond and olive groves that provide engaging backdrops. The sense of disconnection from urban life due to the low population density also enhances the peaceful outdoor experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Tírig?
A: For families, the easier routes are recommended. The region offers 5 easy touring cycling routes, which are generally shorter and have less elevation gain. These routes provide a great way for families to enjoy the scenic landscapes of Tírig together without too much difficulty.
tips: Also on the Via Augusta is the 17th-century L'ermita de Santa Barbara.
